issues.title: Stateful Async Client Reused Across Event Loops
issues.symptom: Celery workers or API requests intermittently crash with event loop mismatch errors.
issues.root_cause: A stateful async AI provider client was cached globally and got reused across different event loops.
issues.resolution: Removed unsafe global caching and reinitialized clients per valid loop context.
issues.lesson_learned: Do not globally cache loop-bound async clients in mixed API + worker systems.

issues.title: Beat Scheduler Started on Multiple Worker Replicas
issues.symptom: Periodic cleanup and maintenance tasks execute multiple times in a cluster.
issues.root_cause: Celery Beat was enabled on more than one worker replica.
issues.resolution: Limited Beat startup to a single designated worker instance.
issues.lesson_learned: Distributed schedulers must run as a singleton.

issues.title: Redis-Dependent Objects Created at Import Time
issues.symptom: API startup crashes or route import failures when Redis is not ready.
issues.root_cause: Redis-backed objects such as rate limiters were constructed at module import time.
issues.resolution: Moved construction to dependency injection / runtime factory paths.
issues.lesson_learned: Avoid side-effectful infrastructure initialization at import time.

issues.title: Reserved SQLAlchemy Attribute Name
issues.symptom: Application startup fails with SQLAlchemy InvalidRequestError related to `metadata`.
issues.root_cause: An ORM model used `metadata` as a mapped Python attribute name.
issues.resolution: Renamed the Python attribute to `artifact_metadata` while preserving DB column mapping where needed.
issues.lesson_learned: Never map reserved SQLAlchemy names directly as ORM attributes.

issues.title: Qdrant Document ID Drift
issues.symptom: Upload appears successful but retrieval/delete by document ID fails or returns zero matches.
issues.root_cause: LlamaIndex auto-generated ref_doc_id/doc_id values that no longer matched PostgreSQL document IDs.
issues.resolution: Pinned LlamaIndex source document IDs explicitly to the PostgreSQL `document_id`.
issues.lesson_learned: Cross-system joins require explicit stable IDs.

issues.title: Double Chunking in Ingestion
issues.symptom: Sections are fragmented into overly small chunks and retrieval quality drops.
issues.root_cause: Markdown parse logic and the ingestion pipeline both applied chunk splitting.
issues.resolution: Kept chunking in one place only: the ingestion pipeline.

issues.title: Worker Startup Broke Due to Noisy Hardware Detection Output
issues.symptom: Celery worker fails because concurrency/autoscale values are parsed as invalid strings.
issues.root_cause: Third-party hardware libraries printed extra text to stdout during shell command substitution.
issues.resolution: Captured only the final line and validated numeric values before using them.
issues.lesson_learned: Always sanitize subprocess stdout before using it as config.

issues.title: Allowed Origins Textarea Lost Newlines
issues.symptom: Platform admin cannot press Enter to add multiple origins line by line.
issues.root_cause: Frontend normalized `allowed_origins` on every keystroke, immediately collapsing blank newline state.
issues.resolution: Switched to draft textarea state and only parse lines on load/save.
issues.lesson_learned: Keep raw multiline form state until submit time.

issues.title: Frontend Crash on Admin Dashboard Health Card
issues.symptom: The `/admin` page renders a blank error screen with `Object.entries(undefined)`.
issues.resolution: Frontend now supports both shapes and uses safe fallbacks when data is missing.
issues.lesson_learned: Health/status contracts should tolerate additive backend shape changes.

issues.title: Root Docs Drifted Away From Actual Project State
issues.symptom: README, AGENTS, and architecture docs describe single-tenant, TEI-only, session-based behavior that no longer exists.
issues.root_cause: Large refactor landed faster than top-level documentation updates.
issues.resolution: Rewrote root docs to match the current multi-tenant, stateless, OpenAI-compatible architecture.
issues.lesson_learned: Large refactors need documentation closure before release tagging.
